steps.title: Find the independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence first says Okinaka does not make decisions single-handedly. It then gives a full second clause explaining that all historical designations must be approved by nine other experts. The transition however can close the first sentence before the second begins.

steps.title: Avoid the comma splice
steps.content: Choice A places however between independent clauses with only commas. Choice B adds an incoherent and after the transition. Choice C lacks the punctuation needed after however. Ending the first sentence after however and starting a new sentence with All is grammatical. The answer is D.
